title: ActiveMQ Broker Memory Limit Pressure
app: Splunk Add-on for JMX (Splunkbase 2647) or scripted Jolokia→HEC; JMS Messaging Modular Input (Splunkbase 1317) for message ingress patterns
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:broker"
| eval mem_pct=coalesce(MemoryPercentUsage, memory_percent_usage)
| where mem_pct > 70
| timechart span=5m max(mem_pct) as broker_memory_pct by broker_name
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Broker memory percent tracks usage against the broker-wide memory limit before producers are blocked or messages are spooled to temp storage.
value: Prevents broker-wide flow control and slow-consumer spirals by catching memory pressure before the store or temp limits are hit.
grandmaExplanation: We help you prevent broker-wide flow control and slow-consumer spirals by catching memory pressure before the store or temp limits are hit.
implementation: Poll broker MBean every 60s via JMX. Map attribute names to Splunk fields (`MemoryPercentUsage` is common on Classic brokers). Correlate with slow consumer advisories and destination memory usage. Alert at 70% sustained 10 minutes; tune per broker sizing.
visualization: Gauge (current memory %), Line chart (memory % trend), Table (brokers over threshold).
knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
